_comment: Copy this to statusline.config.json in the same folder as statusline.js. Your overrides deep-merge over the built-in defaults, so you only need the keys you actually change. Easiest way to edit: `node statusline.js --config`. 256-color codes: https://www.ditig.com/256-colors-cheat-sheet
_guardian: The next block powers the guardian, which is ON by default (autopilot:notify). It checkpoints your work + sends a desktop ping at limits, but never launches anything unattended. Set autopilot:off for a bar-only, side-effect-free install. --auto (or autopilot:resume) adds hands-free relaunch; keepWorking + resume need the hooks wired by --install-guardian.
_autopilotBypassPermissions: Opt-in: let the unattended auto-resume relaunch bypass permission prompts (it is headless and cannot answer one). Off by default; only affects the guardian's own relaunch.
_updatePubkey: Empty = TLS-only updates (default). Paste an Ed25519 PUBLIC key (PEM) to REQUIRE a signed statusline.js.sig on every --update. See the statusline.js header for the openssl commands.
_profileLabels: Map a config-dir name to a label, e.g. {".claude":"work",".claude-personal":"personal"}. Default {} derives labels from the dir name.
